notes: Strong conditional claim with named entity (Hormozi), two clear conditions (agency serves CAC needs, operator masters AI automation), load-bearing mechanism (demand is universal, constraint is operational), bounded by realistic domain limits.

Mechanism claim with specific perk examples (no Saturday nights, better truck, parking), clear mechanism (title alone emotionally discounted, perk creates persistent reminder), well-bounded to expanded career ladders and frontline roles.

Comparison claim with named entities (Panda Express, Alex Hormozi), two anchors (27% margin for 3,000-unit chain, ≥27% implied for independent), falsifiable on margin benchmarks, bounded by business model (QSR vs. artisan).

Assertion about scope creep across three avatars (end-customers, practitioners, business owners), mechanism clear (parallel micro-businesses with separate funnels), bounded by revenue ceiling ($100M+). Load-bearing for service business strategy.

Quantified claim with three anchors (70%, 80%, 90% gross margins), clear axis (gross margin vs. available operating budget), mechanism is sound (gross margin ceiling), bounded by commodity vs. decommoditized services. Falsifiable via margin math.

Mechanism claim about direct-response doom loop, well-conditioned ($30M-$40M, national/international, paid media focus, 2024-2025 context), falsifiable on CAC scaling, bounded by drop-ship/local business exceptions and regulated categories. Load-bearing for marketing strategy.

Assertion about deal flow generation distinct from organic growth, conditions specified (buy-and-hold acquirer, SaaS verticals), falsifiable on outbound/conference conversion vs. organic growth, load-bearing for acquisition strategy.

Mechanism claim about evolutionary pessimism bias, attributed to Hormozi with Bezos as source credit, falsifiable through evolutionary biology and cognitive science, bounded acknowledgment of mismatch between ancestral and modern environments. Load-bearing for understanding risk-taking behavior.

Assertion about impulse control as trait of hyper-successful people, named speaker (Hormozi), falsifiable through personality/behavioral measurement, load-bearing for trait-based success modeling. Generic term 'hyper successful people' mitigated by speaker_term and context.

Mechanism claim about transparent pricing in vendor arbitrage, conditions specified (business owner agreed to discount, founder earned trust), mechanism clear (removes adversarial dynamic, builds goodwill), bounded by Hormozi's evolution (old vs. new approach). Falsifiable on conflict reduction and relationship quality.

Assertion about med spa channel optimization, condition specified (high-demand market like Austin, TX), mechanism clear (organic disguises absence of repeatable system), bounded by favorable vs. unfavorable supply-demand conditions. Falsifiable through competitive testing.

Assertion with quantified axis (700 leads/day, 20 booked calls/day, 0.3% conversion), specific conditions (closers at capacity, DM funnel lead source), mechanism sound (DM setters are capacity ceiling), bounded by show/qualification rate scenarios. Highly falsifiable via funnel math.

Mechanism claim about two-sided monetization for BCBA test-prep, conditions specified (certification + employer demand, graduate volume, institutional relationships), mechanism clear (gateway to scarce talent pool generates employer-side revenue), bounded by execution timing deferral. Load-bearing for test-prep business model.
